steps.title: Express the relative areas of the two squares
steps.content:
Let the smaller square have side length $s$. The larger square has side length $3s$.

Their areas are:
  • Smaller square: $s^2$
  • Larger square: $(3s)^2 = 9s^2$
  • Combined: $10s^2$
The larger square accounts for $\frac{9s^2}{10s^2} = \frac{9}{10}$ of the total area.

steps.title: Calculate the flux through the larger square
steps.content:
Since the electric field is uniform and perpendicular across the surface, the flux is proportional to the area. The flux through the larger square is $\frac{9}{10}$ of the total:
$$\Phi_{\text{larger}} = \frac{9}{10} \times 4,640 = 4,176 \text{ volts}\cdot\text{meters}$$
